description: Letter from Moshe b. Levi ha-Levi, Qalyūb, addressed to his father, the cantor Abu Sahl (Levi) in Fustat. Moshe reports on the receipt of various goods. He asks if a woman in the family ('al-kabirah') wants him to buy a certain fabric from Abu l-Yusr. He complains that the only letter he has received recently is one in which his brother told him not to be a silly fool, and he is very upset because he doesn't know what he did to deserve that. Moshe sent with the bearer of the letter some piyyutim ("yotzer"s) for Rosh Hashanah; he had previously requested the "mizmor" for Rosh Hashana but no longer needs it because he found it. He says, "Let me know if Abu l-Khayr arrives." He is upset that his brother had promised to come spend Shabbat with him, but he did not come. He sends regards to his paternal uncles 'Imran and Bayan and reiterates that he needs to know if 'al-kabirah' wants the fabric. Verso contains the address (in Arabic) and Psalm 98 in the handwriting of Abu Sahl Levi (the letter's addressee). See also Mediterranean Society, II, pp. 221, 569. ASE.
tags: Moshe b. Levi, Qalyub, Abu Sahl Levi
description_he: מכתב ממשה ב' לוי הלוי בקליוב לאביו החזן אבו סהל (לוי) בפסטאט. משה מדווח על קבלת סחורות שונות, שואל אם קרובת משפחה רוצה שיקנה בד מסוים, ומתלונן על מכתב עלוב שקיבל מאחיו. הוא שלח פיוטים (יוצרות) לראש השנה ומבקש עדכון אם אבו אל-ח'יר מגיע. בגב: הכתובת ותהילים צ"ח בכתב ידו של אבו סהל לוי.
